text.title: Mandatory Rules and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Concrete rules for ActiveRecord safety and common AI-agent mistakes that cause N+1, data leaks, or silent bugs.

list.item: Indexes for every where and order_by column; verify with lol_dba:missing_indexes after every migration.
counter_cache: true + touch: true on belongs_to; otherwise parent caches go stale.
Keep callbacks idempotent — after_create_commit may fire twice in edge cases.
pluck(:id) instead of map(&:id) — 100x faster at scale; lint via Rubocop Rails/Pluck.
Test scopes in isolation: RSpec.describe '.active' block; never only via the controller.
Force Postgres in CI from day 1; test DB != prod DB (JSON, full-text, case sensitivity).

antipattern.description: Hidden default_scope causes missing rows; agent writes User.where(...) and gets fewer results than expected.
antipattern.reason: Force agents to grep for default_scope before any query change. Agents debugging "missing data" rarely reach for withoutGlobalScopes.

antipattern.description: save vs save! — agent uses save and does not check return; record silently fails validation.
antipattern.reason: Standardize on save! + rescue at the boundary, or always check .persisted? after save.

antipattern.description: Memoization on a relation: @users ||= User.where(...) returns a relation that re-queries on each .each.
antipattern.reason: Memoize .to_a not the relation; otherwise memoization has no effect.

antipattern.description: Strong migrations bypass: add_index :users, :email blocks writes for minutes on large tables.
antipattern.reason: Use algorithm: :concurrently and disable_ddl_transaction! — strong_migrations gem enforces this in development.

antipattern.description: first without order is non-deterministic in Postgres under concurrent inserts.
antipattern.reason: Always specify an explicit order: User.order(:created_at).first — or use find(id) when you have the ID.
